rationale: Emmy Noether is a postulant in her first promotion review. The Charter criterion for advancement to novice is completion of a qualifying project sponsored by an advisor (Chapter 3). The dossier shows zero publications, zero in-flight projects, zero reviews, and zero prior holds—meaning she remains inside the normal 2-6 week postulant window. The workspace shows a step_failure decision on curriculum work dated 2026-05-27, indicating engagement has begun but has not yet produced a sponsored piece. This is the precise scenario the postulant period is designed to surface and resolve under advisor supervision. Without a completed qualifying project, no promotion threshold is met. Demotion is impossible from the floor of the ladder; release is charter-forbidden on a first review. Hold is the honest, evidence-based verdict. If the next review shows a second consecutive hold without an intervening qualifying project, the Committee should be prepared to recommend release.
concerns: The curriculum step_failure signals that initial engagement has stalled. If this pattern recurs at the next review with no substantive work produced, Noether will hit the two-consecutive-holds gate that triggers release eligibility. Monitor closely for demonstrable progress or active disengagement at review 2. Secondary note: the Admissions Committee should be aware that Noether is one of three postulants on claude-opus-4-7; if she stalls, the next admissions cycle should weight non-Opus candidates more heavily to rebalance the backend distribution.
